meta_title: For Students: debugging with AI to Solve no version control (Beginner Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to debugging with AI and overcome no version control. A Beginner-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Debugging with AI to Solve No Version Control
content.seo_description: Learn how to use AI debugging to overcome lack of version control. A beginner-level guide using Antigravity and Claude Code to accelerate your dev journey.
content.answer_block: Without version control, a single bug can destroy hours of work. By leveraging AI-driven debugging, you can surgically isolate errors, generate automated rollback points, and refactor code safely. Using tools like Claude Code and Antigravity, you transform your local environment into a resilient, intelligent workspace that protects your progress.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a screen of red error text, the cursor blinking in the void of a codebase that has no history. One wrong 'delete' or 'refactor' and the progress you made over the last six hours evaporates into thin air. This is the student developer’s nightmare: building without a safety net, where every line of code feels like a gamble against total loss.

But what if your editor could remember what you forgot? By integrating AI-driven debugging, you stop wrestling with the chaos of manual file management and start building with the precision of a senior architect. We are going to turn your local machine into a sophisticated lab where AI acts as your pair programmer, preventing catastrophic loss and accelerating your learning curve.

steps.title: Establish an AI-Driven Baseline
steps.description: Before changing code, use AI to generate a 'state-snapshot' of your current logic to ensure you can revert if a feature breaks.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the current project state and generate a concise summary of the core logic, including all active variable dependencies and potential failure points.

steps.title: Isolate the Bug with Semantic Search
steps.description: Stop guessing where the error is. Use AI to scan your entire directory to find the precise file causing the regression.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Perform a semantic search across the project to identify the source of the recent runtime error, ignoring standard library files.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Identify the specific line causing this runtime error based on the latest stack trace.'

steps.title: Generate a Surgical Fix
steps.description: Avoid rewriting entire files. Apply targeted patches that fix the issue without introducing side effects in your unversioned codebase.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Propose a minimal code change to resolve the identified bug, ensuring the fix is backward compatible with existing logic.
steps.claude_code_command: claude apply --plan 'Fix the identified function logic to handle null edge cases'

steps.title: Automated Regression Testing
steps.description: Since you lack Git, use AI to write a one-off test script that verifies the fix works before you continue building.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a temporary test script that validates the new fix and ensures the primary feature functionality remains intact.

steps.title: Architectural Documentation Snapshot
steps.description: Document your changes in a local 'decisions.md' file via AI so you have a roadmap of what you changed, acting as a manual version history.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Summarize the changes made today and append them to a local documentation file, noting the logic implemented for future reference.

pro_tips: Always use a 'sandbox' sub-directory when testing high-risk refactors to keep your main files clean.
Maintain a 'decisions.md' file that AI keeps updated; it serves as a lightweight, human-readable version history.
Use AI to generate 'undo' scripts—small snippets that revert specific variable changes—before modifying complex logic.
Leverage Claude Code’s 'read' capability to keep the AI context updated with your latest file changes manually if you aren't using Git.
Treat your AI chat history as a log; export your debugging sessions to store your thought process for future portfolio interviews.
content.next_steps: Now that you have secured your workflow, move toward professional standards by initializing a Git repository immediately. Use the 'decisions.md' file you created as the foundation for your first commit message.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools to stop losing code and start shipping features. Don't let the lack of version control hold back your potential. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
